title: polygala welwitschii subsp. welwitschii
intro: Polygala welwitschii subsp. welwitschii, also known as the 'Khai-Noi' or 'Miao-Hua' plant, is a traditional medicinal herb used in various cultures. It is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae, native to Southeast Asia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential use in managing diabetes and cardiovascular disease. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or tea, with the roots and leaves being used separately or in combination to make medicinal infusions.
